rulings.Case Title: CHEUK TIN YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs From Deft Tony Fu Pursuant To Ccp Sec 425.16(C)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 17, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFF CHEUK YAN Motion for Attorneys Fees and Costs from Defendant Tony Fu Pursuant To CCP Sec 425.16(C). Motion for attorney fees granted. Fu's motion was totally and completely without merit and his counsel should have withdrawn it before the hearing. However, the claimed fees are grossly inflated in view of the four page opposition and the simplicity of this fee motion. The Court finds counsel's hourly rate of $280.00 to be reasonable. The court finds eight hours for work related to the anti-Slapp motion and six hours for work related to the fees motion to be reasonable and necessary, resulting in a lodestar amount of $3,920 to be reasonable attorney fees. The Court denies the request for $100 in delivery charges as unnecessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BANK SAL. OPPENHEIM JR & CIE (SCHWEIZ) AG VS. PLANTAGENET CAPITAL FUND, L.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 17, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFFS BANK OPPENHEIM, CIE AG Motion To Vacate Arbitration. Motion denied. The court considers conduct that occurred after Judge Alvarado's order and the current motion is not a motion to reconsider that order. AAA rules adequately address costs. Defendants' refusal to share costs does not constitute a waiver of their right to arbitrate as such refusal is not contrary to any of the costs provisions provided in the agreement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 17, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS KAREN SARID, SHELLA CERVANTES, MARK CERVANTES, CHI-LEUNG CHENG, ALICE CHANG, RACHEL GISHKEN, THEODORE GRADMAN DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The 6th cause of action for money had and received is sufficiently pled: defendants promised a lower interest rate, charged a higher interest rate, and plaintiff paid that higher rate. The 7th and 8th causes of action are derivitive claims that survive demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RITA RABINOVICH VS. COUNTRIWIDE HOME LOANS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 17, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS INC, COUNTRYWIDE HOME LOANS, INC., RECONTRUST COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Punitive damages must be pled with specificity. B&P sec. 17200 action is equitable in nature and damages cannot be recovered. Plaintiff must allege valid claim for treble damages and attorney fees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RITA RABINOVICH VS. COUNTRIWIDE HOME LOANS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 17, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS INC, COUNTRYWIDE HOME LOANS, INC., RECONTRUST COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The 1st cause of action (quiet title) alleges that MERS and Recontrust did not have authority to foreclose which is contradicted by the properly judicially noticed deed of trust. The 2nd cause of action (wrongful foreclosure) must allege an ability and willingness to tender full payment. (Abdullah vs. U.S. Bank (1996) 43 Cal.App. 4th 1101.) As to the 3rd cause of action (fraudulent misrepresentation) plaintiff must plead a legally recognized duty and justifiable reliance. Plaintiff must overcome the general rule that there is not duty between a lending institution and a borrower. (Nymark vs. Heart Fed. & Loan Assoc. Sav. (1191) 231 Cal.App. 3rd 1089.) As to the 4th cause of action (implied covenant) plaintiff must plead substantive duties that are not beyond those incorporated into the terms of the parties contract. (Agosta vs. Astor (2004) 120 Cal.App. 4th 596.) The 5th cause of action (B&P sec. 17200) is predicated on the fraud claim which fails for lack of specificity, and for failure to plead a duty or justifiable reliance. =(302/CWW)
